title: cutandia dichotoma var. memphitica
intro: Cutandia dichotoma var. memphitica, also known as Mexican needle grass, is a perennial grass species that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been attributed to its essential oils and other bioactive compounds. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Cutandia dichotoma var. memphitica can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
